testcases.input: aliceValues = [1,3], bobValues = [2,1] Output: 1 Explanation: If Alice takes stone 1 (0-indexed) first, Alice will receive 3 points. Bob can only choose stone 0, and will only receive 2 points. Alice wins.
testcases.expectedOutput: 1 Explanation: If Alice takes stone 1 (0-indexed) first, Alice will receive 3 points. Bob can only choose stone 0, and will only receive 2 points. Alice wins.

testcases.input: aliceValues = [1,2], bobValues = [3,1] Output: 0 Explanation: If Alice takes stone 0, and Bob takes stone 1, they will both have 1 point. Draw.
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: If Alice takes stone 0, and Bob takes stone 1, they will both have 1 point. Draw.

testcases.input: aliceValues = [2,4,3], bobValues = [1,6,7] Output: -1 Explanation: Regardless of how Alice plays, Bob will be able to have more points than Alice. For example, if Alice takes stone 1, Bob can take stone 2, and Alice takes stone 0, Alice will have 6 points to Bob's 7. Bob wins.
testcases.expectedOutput: -1 Explanation: Regardless of how Alice plays, Bob will be able to have more points than Alice. For example, if Alice takes stone 1, Bob can take stone 2, and Alice takes stone 0, Alice will have 6 points to Bob's 7. Bob wins.
